title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. typica
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. typica, also known as common wormwood or European wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. This perennial herb has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of artemisia vulgaris subsp. typica include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infection. Artemisia vulgaris subsp. typica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in aromatherapy and topical applications.
